2604.03199.title: Learning the Signature of Memorization in Autoregressive Language Models
2604.03199.paper_summary: All prior membership inference attacks for fine-tuned language models use hand-crafted heuristics (e.g., loss thresholding, Min-K\%, reference calibration), each bounded by the designer's intuition. We introduce the first transferable learned attack, enabled by the observation that fine-tuning any model on any corpus yields unlimited labeled data, since membership is known by construction. This removes the shadow model bottleneck and brings membership inference into the deep learning era: learning what matters rather than designing it, with generalization through training diversity and scale. We discover that fine-tuning language models produces an invariant signature of memorization detectable across architectural families and data domains. We train a membership inference classifier exclusively on transformer-based models. It transfers zero-shot to Mamba (state-space), RWKV-4 (linear attention), and RecurrentGemma (gated recurrence), achieving 0.963, 0.972, and 0.936 AUC respectively. Each evaluation combines an architecture and dataset never seen during training, yet all three exceed performance on held-out transformers (0.908 AUC). These four families share no computational mechanisms, their only commonality is gradient descent on cross-entropy loss. Even simple likelihood-based methods exhibit strong transfer, confirming the signature exists independently of the detection method. Our method, Learned Transfer MIA (LT-MIA), captures this signal most effectively by reframing membership inference as sequence classification over per-token distributional statistics. On transformers, LT-MIA achieves 2.8$\times$ higher TPR at 0.1\% FPR than the strongest baseline. The method also transfers to code (0.865 AUC) despite training only on natural language texts. Code and trained classifier available at https://github.com/JetBrains-Research/learned-mia.
2604.03199.paper_summary_zh: 所有先前針對微調語言模型的會員推斷攻擊都使用手工製作的啟發式方法（例如，損失閾值、Min-K\%、參考校準），每一種方法都受到設計者直覺的限制。我們引入了第一個可轉移的學習攻擊，這是基於這樣的觀察：在任何語料庫上微調任何模型都會產生無限的標記數據，因為會員身份是由構造決定的。這消除了影子模型的瓶頸，將會員推斷帶入深度學習時代：學習重要的內容，而不是設計它，通過訓練的多樣性和規模實現泛化。我們發現微調語言模型會產生一種不變的記憶簽名，這種簽名可以在不同的架構族和數據領域中檢測到。我們專門在基於Transformer的模型上訓練了一個會員推斷分類器。它在零樣本轉移到 Mamba（狀態空間）、RWKV-4（線性注意力）和 RecurrentGemma（門控遞歸），分別達到 0.963、0.972 和 0.936 的 AUC。每次評估都結合了一種在訓練期間從未見過的架構和數據集，但這三者的表現都超過了在保留Transformer上的表現（0.908 AUC）。這四個家族沒有共享計算機制，它們唯一的共同點是對交叉熵損失的梯度下降。即使是簡單的基於似然的方法也表現出強大的轉移，證實了簽名獨立於檢測方法存在。我們的方法，學習轉移 MIA（LT-MIA），通過將會員推斷重新框定為對每個標記的分佈統計進行序列分類，最有效地捕捉到這一信號。在Transformer上，LT-MIA 在 0.1\% FPR 時達到了比最強基線高 2.8$\times$ 的 TPR。該方法也能轉移到代碼（0.865 AUC），儘管僅在自然語言文本上進行訓練。代碼和訓練的分類器可在 https://github.com/JetBrains-Research/learned-mia 獲得。
2604.03199.author: David Ilić et.al.
2604.03199.authors: David Ilić, Kostadin Cvejoski, David Stanojević, Evgeny Grigorenko
